title: T-125 — Global Import/Export backend jobs and capability registry
prompt:
# T-125 — Global Import/Export backend jobs and capability registry

## Goal
Implement the backend capability registry and job lifecycle for global Settings Import / Export (UX SET-029). The registry tells the UI which entities support import/export, which permissions are required, which template/download routes exist, and whether the operation is sync, async, or unsupported.

This task supports T-121 UI and must not collapse global import/export into reference-table CSV only.

## Files in scope
- packages/server/src/settings/import-export/capability-registry.ts [create]
- packages/server/src/settings/import-export/jobs.ts [create]
- packages/server/src/__tests__/settings-import-export.test.ts [create]
- packages/db/schema/settings-import-export-jobs.ts [create/modify]
- packages/db/migrations/NNN-settings-import-export-jobs.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/settings-import-export-jobs.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given the capability registry is queried, when a user has mixed permissions, then users, roles, invitations, reference tables, infrastructure, feature flags, authorization policies, and audit-log export capabilities are permission-filtered with explicit import/export support labels AND when a reference-table import is requested the capability resolves to T-022/T-096 preview/commit semantics rather than creating a divergent parser.
- Given an unsupported import entity is selected, when an import job is requested, then a typed unsupported-entity blocker is returned and no job or mutation is created.
- Given an authorization-policy import is requested, when preflight runs, then it requires settings.authorization.edit, audit reason, and T-126 dry-run validation; V-SET-43/V-SET-44 cannot be bypassed.
- Given export is requested, when permissions pass, then a read-only job record is created with status/progress/download metadata and org isolation.

Note: AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F8 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost; former AC3 reference-table handoff is a strict subset of AC1 capability-resolution behavior and fused into AC1 with AND clause; original assertion preserved in fused AC text and `test_strategy`).

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: add tests for permission-filtered capabilities, unsupported import blockers, reference handoff, authorization-policy preflight blockers, export job status, and org isolation.
- GREEN: implement registry and job helpers around existing reference/auth/export primitives.
- Run targeted server/db tests plus typecheck/lint for changed packages.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement global import as a thin alias for reference CSV only.
- Do not allow import/export to bypass authorization policy validation, permissions, audit reason, or org isolation.
- Do not silently hide unsupported imports; return explicit typed capability state.
- Do not implement T-121 UI in this backend task.
## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED -> GREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC: pass/fail)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status
